finding_results.notes: Rewrote three Margot speeches in the Helga therapy exchange. Line 95: replaced thesis formulation ('The wanting is the experience' / 'the craving and the fix at the same time') with body-concrete description of plugging in — heartbeat in sternum, diaphragm matching, fear of loss arriving in the first second. Line 101: replaced curated memory speech ('proprioceptive sense of being twenty-one and built for exactly this') with rougher, present-tense body recall — hamstrings loading, eyes locking, self-interruption ('His whole body was just —' She stopped), and a concrete physical proof ('My hands are sweating'). Line 109: replaced aphoristic coexistence diagnosis ('These two things just sit next to each other in my head without canceling out') with blunt scenario declaration ('if you plugged me in right now I'd say yes. I know what it cost him and I'd still say yes'). Line 119 ('Like furniture') left intact as character-true blunt Margot. All revised speeches deliver core facts (live feed vs. recorded, intact memory, craving unchanged) through body-reactive language and rougher syntax rather than composed self-interpretation. Preserved Margot's high contraction level, self-interruption tendency, and flat declarative stress register per style bible.

summary: Single finding addressed: Margot's therapy dialogue replaced composed thesis speech with body-concrete, pressured delivery across three speeches (lines 95, 101, 109). Sensory detail now arrives through re-experienced sensation (heartbeat, hamstring load, sweating hands, spinal compression) rather than articulate addiction diagnosis. Self-interruptions and flat declarations replace balanced aphorisms. No changes to chapter structure, continuity, or non-dialogue passages. Heading contract preserved.
